metadata.title: New Orleans Tours, Things to Do, Food, and Music Guide | Destination Command Center
metadata.description: Discover New Orleans tours, things to do, food experiences, live music, neighborhoods, and trip ideas. Browse swamp tours, ghost tours, food tours, sightseeing, and more.
keywords: things to do in new orleans

hero.title: New Orleans tours, things to do, food, music, and neighborhood guides
hero.summary: Browse bookable New Orleans tours, discover neighborhoods worth your time, and plan around the city's live music, food, and cultural highlights.
hero.trustLine: Find tours, attractions, neighborhoods, live experiences, and trip ideas in New Orleans.

tourCategories.title: Swamp Tours
tourCategories.description: Airboat and bayou outings for wildlife, marsh scenery, and an easy half-day escape from the city.

tourCategories.title: Ghost Tours
tourCategories.description: Night walks through haunted streets, historic buildings, and the city's darker legends.

tourCategories.title: Food Tours
tourCategories.description: Creole classics, neighborhood bites, and local food stories beyond the obvious checklist stops.

tourCategories.title: Jazz & Music Experiences
tourCategories.description: Live music walks, jazz history, and experiences built around the city's signature sound.

topAttractions.title: French Quarter
topAttractions.description: Historic streets, classic architecture, and the city's best-known walking district.

topAttractions.title: Garden District
topAttractions.description: Tree-lined blocks, historic homes, and a slower daytime pace.

topAttractions.title: Frenchmen Street
topAttractions.description: Music-first nights, jazz clubs, and a more local-feeling nightlife corridor.

topAttractions.title: Jackson Square
topAttractions.description: An iconic public square that anchors many first French Quarter walks.

topAttractions.title: Bourbon Street
topAttractions.description: The city's loudest nightlife corridor, best treated as one focused evening lane rather than the whole New Orleans experience.

topAttractions.title: The National WWII Museum
topAttractions.description: A major museum anchor that rewards a dedicated daytime block and works well beyond the French Quarter-heavy itinerary.

featuredTours.description: Popular guided experiences and bookable activities for visitors planning around the city's food, music, neighborhoods, and day trips.

planningLinks.description: How to structure a first New Orleans trip without wasting your best time blocks.

planningLinks.title: What to do this weekend

planningLinks.title: Family-friendly New Orleans
planningLinks.description: Low-stress attractions, daytime activities, and tours that work for mixed-age groups.

planningLinks.title: Food and drink guide

planningLinks.title: Music and nightlife guide

faq.q: What are the best tours in New Orleans for first-time visitors?
faq.a: Many first-time visitors start with a French Quarter walk, a food tour, a jazz or music experience, and one swamp or bayou tour to balance city culture with Louisiana landscapes.

faq.q: How many days do you need in New Orleans?
faq.a: A strong first trip is 3 to 4 days. That gives you time for the French Quarter, at least one neighborhood beyond downtown, a food or music experience, and one day tour or swamp excursion.

faq.q: What area should first-time visitors stay in?
faq.a: Many first-time visitors stay near the French Quarter, Warehouse District, or CBD so they can walk to major sights and keep late-night music, dining, and tour departures simple.
